items.name: Raspberry Pi 4 Model B (4GB)
items.role: Brain — runs the Goose Guard software
items.notes: Any Pi 4 or newer works. 4GB is plenty for audio playback.

items.name: Official USB-C Power Supply (5.1V 3A)
items.notes: Do not use a phone charger — under-powered supplies cause audio glitches and random reboots.

items.name: MicroSD Card (32GB, A1, Class 10)
items.role: Storage for OS and sound library
items.notes: SanDisk Extreme or Samsung Evo Plus are reliable picks. 32GB holds hundreds of deterrent clips.

items.name: Amplified Outdoor Speaker
items.role: Makes the noise the geese hate
items.notes: Any powered speaker with a 3.5mm input works. For larger properties, a horn speaker plus small amplifier gives much longer range.

items.name: 3.5mm Stereo Audio Cable
items.role: Connects the Pi to the speaker
items.notes: Get a length that comfortably reaches from the Pi's sheltered spot to the speaker.

items.name: Raspberry Pi Case
items.notes: If the Pi lives in a shed or under eaves a basic case with a fan is fine. Fully outdoor deployments need an IP-rated enclosure.

items.name: PIR Motion Sensor (HC-SR501)
items.notes: Only needed for Motion mode. Wire VCC→5V, GND→GND, OUT→GPIO17 (changeable in config.json).

items.name: Female-to-Female Jumper Wires
items.role: Wires the PIR sensor to the Pi GPIO pins
items.notes: Three wires is all you need for the PIR; buy a 40-pack for future tinkering.

items.name: Ethernet Cable
items.role: Wired network if Wi-Fi is flaky at the edge of the property
items.notes: Pi 4 has Wi-Fi built in, but wired is more reliable outdoors.
